lines: Wilmot leads the pack on 11pts and Morocco are doing their bit — beating Scotland 1-0 means the four Morocco co-owners (Butters, Callum, Jim, Wilmot) are all quietly smug, though Wilmot's the only one actually benefiting in the standings right now.

Al watched the Netherlands absolutely batter Sweden 5-1 yesterday, which was great for Al and absolutely catastrophic for Jim, who now has Sweden limping on 3pts AND Qatar on 1pt — mate, your Group B situation is giving 'squad depth of a pub team' energy.

The Foreman is in genuine crisis: Türkiye lost to Paraguay, making it two losses from two and officially bottom of Group D with zero points, AND Curaçao sit bottom of Group E with zero points — today Ecuador vs Curaçao is essentially a mercy mission The Foreman cannot afford to lose.
Dene is on 4pts and has Tunisia playing Japan today — a must-win for Tunisia after their opening defeat, meaning it's also a must-watch for Dene, who currently has all the momentum of a parked car.
Watch Group F closely today: if Tunisia (Callum/Dene) beat Japan, suddenly that group gets spicy — but if Japan win, Callum's Tunisia co-ownership becomes as useful as a waterproof teabag.
